text: Patient has history of sepsis/septic shock admission with mechanical ventilation for ARDS

slots.condition: sepsis/septic shock with ARDS on ventilator

transcript_quote: the sepsis admission, septic shock, time on the ventilator with the ARDS

text: Patient reports exertional dyspnea (winded on hills/stairs) but no shortness of breath at rest
transcript_quote: I get winded on hills, and if I try to jog the dog gets embarrassed for me, but at rest I'm fine.

text: Patient reports fatigue/reduced exercise tolerance; stairs remain difficult but walks daily
transcript_quote: Stairs still take something out of me. But I walk every day now.

text: Denies fevers, chills, night sweats since discharge
transcript_quote: No, nothing like that. Knock on wood.

text: Denies chest pain and dyspnea at rest
transcript_quote: No. I get winded on hills... but at rest I'm fine.

text: Denies cough
transcript_quote: Not really. The lungs feel quieter than they used to

text: Denies dizziness or palpitations related to anemia; color reportedly returned around March
transcript_quote: My wife says I finally got my color back around March. No dizziness.

text: History of anemia found during hospitalization, currently asymptomatic, to continue monitoring
transcript_quote: They also found you anemic in the hospital.

text: Reports low back pain when sitting too long, rated 1/10
transcript_quote: The low back mutters when I sit too long. It's background noise.

text: Patient became unemployed in February after layoff following prolonged ICU absence; first time unemployed since age 16
transcript_quote: I got let go in February... So — unemployed. First time since I was sixteen years old.

text: Patient reports social isolation, reduced contact with friends/family; sees close people less than once a week
transcript_quote: The guys from work stopped calling around the time I stopped being useful to them.

text: Housing strain: three-bedroom home with 6 occupants, unresolved landlord repairs; worried about housing

slots.detail: overcrowded, disrepair, worried about losing housing

transcript_quote: It's too small, plain and simple. Three bedrooms, six people.

text: Household income adequate; wife's job supports household; private insurance
transcript_quote: Money-wise we're okay — my wife's job carries the household fine

text: Community resource sheet provided including employment center and tenant advocacy line
transcript_quote: I'll have the front desk print our community resource sheet — there's an employment center and a tenant advocacy line on it.

text: Assessment of health and social care needs completed

slots.procedure: Assessment of health and social care needs

transcript_quote: Part of today's visit is a formal assessment of health and social care needs

text: Depression screening (PHQ-2) performed, total score 1/6, low risk
transcript_quote: That puts you at one point out of six on the short mood screen, which is low

text: Denies illicit drug use in past 12 months; DAST-10 score 0
transcript_quote: Your drug screening questionnaire comes out to a zero

text: Never smoker; minimal alcohol (occasional beer)
transcript_quote: Tobacco, ever? PT: Never.

text: Substance use assessment / drug abuse screening performed

slots.procedure: Assessment of substance use / screening for drug abuse

text: Patient takes no daily prescription medications; hospital medications tapered off at follow-ups
transcript_quote: Day to day? Nothing regular. When I left the hospital they whittled the list down and then off entirely

text: No known medication allergies
transcript_quote: None that anyone's ever found.

text: Medication reconciliation completed to remove residual hospital medications
transcript_quote: Then I'll reconcile the list — it was still showing some hospital leftovers

text: Blood pressure 107/69 mmHg
transcript_quote: Your blood pressure today is 107 over 69, which is excellent.

text: Heart rate 63/min
transcript_quote: Heart rate 63

text: Respiratory rate 15/min
transcript_quote: breathing 15 times a minute

text: Exam: lungs clear on deep breathing, heart sounds regular, no ankle edema
transcript_quote: Heart sounds regular. Let me see the ankles... no swelling.

text: Weight 84.4 kg, height 166.8 cm, BMI 30.34 (obesity)
transcript_quote: Weight came in at 84.4 kilos, height 166.8, so the BMI works out to 30.34 — just over the obesity line.

text: Hemoglobin A1c 5.99% - consistent with prediabetes
transcript_quote: The hemoglobin A1c... is 5.99 percent. That keeps you in prediabetes territory.

text: Fasting/random blood glucose 86.7 mg/dL - normal
transcript_quote: your glucose today was 86.7, completely normal

text: Basic metabolic panel: Sodium 139, Potassium 4.89, Chloride 104, CO2 28, normal
transcript_quote: Sodium 139, potassium 4.89, chloride and bicarbonate fine.

text: Kidney function normal after septic shock: creatinine 1.22 mg/dL, BUN 13.78 mg/dL
transcript_quote: Kidneys came through at a creatinine of 1.22 with a BUN of 13.78

text: Prediabetes managed with lifestyle: 5-7% weight loss (~4-6 kg) and increased daily walking

slots.target: 5-7% weight loss, daily walking 30 min most days

transcript_quote: Five to seven percent weight loss — for you that's roughly four to six kilos — plus stretching that daily walk out

text: Counseled on daily walking routine ~30 minutes most days for metabolic, weight and mood benefit
transcript_quote: I'd rather see thirty minutes most days than one heroic hour on Saturday.

text: Influenza vaccine (trivalent, split virus, PF) administered in left arm; missed fall flu shot

slots.drug: influenza vaccine, split virus, trivalent, PF

transcript_quote: it's the standard trivalent one, sore arm for a day at most

text: No prior adverse reaction to influenza vaccine
transcript_quote: No, I just forget them.

text: Plan to recheck HbA1c in approximately 6 months
transcript_quote: recheck the A1c in about six months

text: Follow-up appointment to be booked at checkout; continue monitoring anemia and mood

slots.instruction: schedule follow-up, monitor anemia and mood

transcript_quote: the front desk will book your follow-up

text: Counseled patient to report any worsening mood to physician
transcript_quote: if that changes, you tell me. Not the dog, me.

text: Fatigue attributed to combination of anemia, lung recovery, and unemployment; further workup if it plateaus or worsens
transcript_quote: Probably a share of each, and all three should keep improving. If the fatigue plateaus or gets worse instead, we look harder at it.

text: Medication review flagged as due, now resolved
Serum calcium 9.98 mg/dL - normal
PRAPARE assessment: feels safe at home, no partner fear, stress level not at all, housing insecurity concern present

slots.detail: safe housing, low stress, worried about losing housing
